spec_match.rationale: Two synthesised tones share a frame-default `setPreferredSinkId`, with per-element `audio.setSinkId()` shown overriding the default for one track. That precedence is exactly what the spec defines.

interactive.rationale: Mixing-desk UI with select + 7 listeners drives real per-track audio output device routing. Working app, not a card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index says `Two synthesised tones, one frame-default sink, and per-element overrides. Watch the frame default route every audio element — then watch audio.setSinkId() beat the default for one track`; page implements this faithfully.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers the precedence override well but never demonstrates removing the per-element override (going back to frame default), nor the case where the chosen device is unplugged mid-playback.

references.rationale: `see also` now cites `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5098834847072256` and the Audio Output Devices API spec.

design_system.rationale: 35 CSS variable references, no raw hex, no button override mismatch.

openQuestions.title: Add a feature-detect probe
openQuestions.detail: Unlike the sibling pages, this concept has no `typeof navigator.mediaDevices.setPreferredSinkId === 'function'` probe. Add one at the top so users see whether their browser exposes the method.

openQuestions.title: Show clearing a per-element override
openQuestions.detail: After `audio.setSinkId(...)` an element no longer follows the frame default. Add a `reset to frame default` button per track that calls `setSinkId('')` and confirms the element resumes following the frame setting.

openQuestions.title: Handle device-unplug mid-playback
openQuestions.detail: Listen for `navigator.mediaDevices.devicechange` and surface a banner when the active sink disappears — the precedence demo silently breaks if the chosen device is removed.

summary: Real mixing-desk-style demo of the per-element-overrides-frame-default precedence rule, with the required ChromeStatus/spec references now present. A feature-detect probe, reset-override action, and devicechange handling would round it out.
